acceptance_criteria: Profile loader deserializes a fixture profile.yaml containing a `research:` block into the typed model.
Unknown keys under `research:` do not throw.
Profile-schema documentation updated with the new block and one example per platform.
Unit tests cover happy-path deserialization and each documented field.
`dotnet build --no-restore` and `dotnet test --no-restore` pass with zero warnings.
description:
Introduce a typed model for the `research:` block in `.polyphony-config/profile.yaml` and a YamlDotNet-based loader.

**Scope**
- New record(s) under `src/Polyphony/Configuration/`: `ProfileConfig` (if not already present — profile.yaml is currently only existence-checked at [src/Polyphony/Configuration/ConfigValidator.cs](src/Polyphony/Configuration/ConfigValidator.cs):240) plus nested `ResearchConfig`, `ResearchAuthConfig`.
- Fields: `repo` (string), `branch` (string, default `main`), `platform` (enum `github`|`ado`), `auth: { kind: env|gh-cli|ado-cli, env_var?: string }`.
- Loader uses YamlDotNet (consistent with `ProcessConfigLoader.cs`); AOT-safe code paths only.
- Sealed records, primary constructors, source-gen JSON if any JSON crosses a boundary.
- Unknown keys tolerated (forward-compat) — do not throw on extra fields.
- Update [docs/polyphony-process-config-schema.md](docs/polyphony-process-config-schema.md) (or the profile-schema doc) with the new block and one worked example per platform.

**Out of scope**
- Validation rules (task-2).
- Storage abstraction (task-3).
title: Add typed `research:` schema + profile loader

acceptance_criteria: Validator emits errors for each documented malformed shape with actionable messages.
Validator emits a warning when `research:` is absent.
Stable `V-N` rule codes assigned; no collisions with existing V-1–V-21 family.
Unit tests cover each new rule (one positive + one negative case minimum).
`dotnet build --no-restore` and `dotnet test --no-restore` pass with zero warnings.
description:
Extend [src/Polyphony/Configuration/ConfigValidator.cs](src/Polyphony/Configuration/ConfigValidator.cs) with rules for the new `research:` block, following the existing `V-N` family and the validator-as-oracle principle (P8).

**Scope**
- New error rules (next free `V-N` codes; coordinate with retired V-17/V-18 — see open question on the parent plan):
  - `research:` present but missing `repo`.
  - `research:` present but missing `platform`.
  - `research.platform` not in {`github`, `ado`}.
  - `research:` present but missing `auth.kind`.
  - `research.auth.kind: env` without `env_var`.
- New warning rule:
  - `research:` absent — research features disabled (informational only).
- All messages must name the offending field path and suggest the fix (P8).
- Wire rules into the existing `Validate(ProcessConfig, repoRoot)` entrypoint by extending the type / passing the loaded `ProfileConfig` through; pick the smallest plumbing change consistent with current style.

**Out of scope**
- The schema model (task-1).
- Storage abstraction (task-3).
title: Add validator rules for the `research:` block

acceptance_criteria: `IResearchStorage` interface defined with `WriteFileAsync` and `ReadFileAsync` returning structured result types.
`GitHubResearchStorage` and `AdoResearchStorage` implementations exist and route through existing `IGhClient` / `IAdoClient`.
`ResearchStorageFactory` returns the correct impl for each `platform` value and throws an actionable error for unsupported values.
Unit tests cover (a) factory selection per platform, (b) each impl's read/write happy path with mocked underlying client, (c) error propagation.
No live network calls in tests.
`dotnet build --no-restore` and `dotnet test --no-restore` pass with zero warnings.
description:
Introduce a narrow research-storage seam that follows polyphony's paired-client convention (mirroring [IGhClient](src/Polyphony/Infrastructure/Processes/IGhClient.cs) / [IAdoClient](src/Polyphony/Infrastructure/AzureDevOps/IAdoClient.cs)), so Issues 2–6 can read and write to a sibling research repo on either platform without per-repo code changes.

**Scope**
- New interface `IResearchStorage` (suggested under `src/Polyphony/Infrastructure/Research/`) with the minimum operations sufficient for the downstream pipeline:
  - `Task<ResearchWriteResult> WriteFileAsync(string path, string content, string commitMessage, CancellationToken)`
  - `Task<ResearchReadResult> ReadFileAsync(string path, CancellationToken)`
- Implementations:
  - `GitHubResearchStorage` — delegates to `IGhClient` (use `gh api` for contents endpoints; reuse retry/policy patterns).
  - `AdoResearchStorage` — delegates to `IAdoClient` Git Items API (mirrors GH semantics).
- Factory `ResearchStorageFactory` selects impl from `ResearchConfig.Platform`.
- All public APIs return structured result types (per project conventions).
- Sealed records / classes, primary constructors, source-gen JSON, AOT-safe.

**Out of scope**
- Any caller wiring (Issues 2–6 will consume this).
- Live API calls in tests — use NSubstitute for `IGhClient`/`IAdoClient`.
title: Build `IResearchStorage` cross-platform abstraction (GitHub + ADO impls)
